name: Eddie "Lockjaw" Davis
abstract: Edward F. Davis (March 2, 1922 – November 3, 1986), known professionally as Eddie "Lockjaw" Davis, was an American jazz tenor saxophonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: That was Columbia. And here's Apollo Records, with Eddie Davis. 'Licks Apollo' Lips of Plenty with me, Gene Ramsey, Al Haig, Denzel Best, and John Collins.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Lockjaw Davis, Ben Webster, Eddie Miller and I went on this thing called Just for Tenor, you know, it was a concert, we did about eight concerts

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Davis and I, we travelled together for about six, seven years... He was looking for a partner and I was too... we made a good team.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play Monday nights, battle of the saxophones, Eddie “Lockjaw” Davis and myself and I played there Monday night
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jillean Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Lockjaw Davis, Marshall Royal of course was always there... and one of our greater friends in the band was Benny Powell
about_quotes.doc_title: Jillean Williams interviewed by Milt Fillius and Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 20, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I think I inherited Eddie “Lockjaw” Davis’s seat... we were both members of the Basie band in 1953
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
